response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems software developer with a broad background in web, mobile, and embedded development, but his experience is entirely in application-layer and backend software engineering. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, and AI accelerator performance analysis — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant work is low-latency D-language development at Sociomantic and some embedded/robotic work at Altom, but neither approaches the hardware architecture or deep-learning workload optimization competencies this role requires.
strengths: 30+ years of software engineering experience demonstrating strong technical longevity and adaptability across domains
Low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic using async I/O, fibers, and high-performance databases indicates performance-minded thinking
Embedded and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, C/C++ components for NI LabWindows) shows some proximity to hardware-level software
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the core technical requirement of the role is entirely absent from the resume
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator optimization experience; deep learning workload expertise is also absent
Educational background (BS Computer Science + experience in web/full-stack) does not align with the hardware engineering or advanced architecture depth expected at principal level for this role
